basics.name: Mike Blom
basics.tagline: Software engineering leader — self-taught engineer, US Army infantry veteran, twelve years building teams and products in education technology.
basics.summary: I grew up in the Netherlands, served in the United States Army infantry with deployments to Iraq and Afghanistan, taught myself to write software in 2014, and spent the next twelve years in education technology growing from engineer into engineering leadership. I lead teams through ambiguity and change, and I build software the way this site was built: with discipline, transparency, and AI as a force multiplier.

chapters.title: Youth in the Netherlands
chapters.summary: Growing up Dutch: a paper route and other odd jobs taught responsibility, independence, and the habit of showing up every day.

chapters.title: US Army Infantry
chapters.summary: Moved to the United States and served in the US Army infantry, deploying to Iraq and Afghanistan. Learned leadership, accountability, and how to perform under pressure.

chapters.title: Software Engineering & EdTech Leadership
chapters.summary: Self-taught software engineer who grew into engineering leadership over twelve years in education technology: building teams, setting technical strategy, and using AI to raise the bar for how software gets built.

chapters.title: The Unwritten Future
chapters.summary: The next chapter isn't written yet. Engineering leadership, AI-focused product development, building companies — let's talk.

experience.title: Engineering leadership roles
experience.summary: Twelve years in education technology, progressing from self-taught software engineer to engineering leadership. Detailed roles, employers, teams, and metrics will be filled in from the content questionnaire.
highlights: Progressed from individual contributor to engineering leadership
Led teams through ambiguity, growth, and organizational change

military.summary: Served in the US Army infantry with deployments to Iraq and Afghanistan. Public details are limited to what has been explicitly approved for publication.
